taxonomy: Polypteris latifolia belongs to the family Polypteridae, which is a group of ancient and primitive bony fishes known as bichirs. The species Polypteris latifolia is classified within the genus Polypteris, which is characterized by its long, eel-like body and distinctive gill slits. The family Polypteridae is part of the order Polypteriformes, a group of primitive fish that has remained relatively unchanged over millions of years. Polypteris latifolia is a freshwater species found in Africa, and its classification reflects its unique and ancient evolutionary history. The species is closely related to other bichirs within the Polypteridae family, which are also found in Africa.
